other_observers: none; I found the two birds on this date.
specific_location: Headquarters Pond at Chico Basin Ranch on the pond's southeast end.
description: This is a reddish bronze ibis with dark eye and dull but glittering bronze green wings. The outstanding feature was the very narrow bluish lores, two parallel lines come above and below the dark eye (to about the center of the eye) but do not completely extend behind and surround the eye. The bill was horn color, long and down curved. The upper leg color dark gray and the knees did not have any rufous color on them. The lower legs below the knees and feet were in the water. The tail was short and bronzy shinning greenish.Behaviors: Resting and foraging with bills down in shallow water near a known point for loafing migrants. Also seen in flight, where they circled many times before landing again.Call: nasal soft flight calls given by whole flock including White-faced Ibis while circling reservoirPlumage: adult alternate
